metadata.title: The Boondoggle Score: Label Who Does Each Step

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: The 'read it back out loud' moment landed for me, because I default to Claude-only on every row too. For a spam classifier build I want to fill in the five-column Score honestly. Help me: (1) identify which steps are essential complexity in Brooks's sense, like choosing the method and setting the threshold; (2) rewrite those rows as human-only or Claude-with-review with a real reason; (3) draft a non-vague handoff condition for the threshold step so the Score can't lie.

props.command: The 'read it back out loud' moment landed for me, because I default to Claude-only on every row too. For a spam classifier build I want to fill in the five-column Score honestly. Help me: (1) identify which steps are essential complexity in Brooks's sense, like choosing the method and setting the threshold; (2) rewrite those rows as human-only or Claude-with-review with a real reason; (3) draft a non-vague handoff condition for the threshold step so the Score can't lie.
props.segment: The Boondoggle Score: Label Who Does

beats.narration_text: He ran the /boondoggle command before starting his build. The Score table appeared: five steps, a labor column for each. He filled it in quickly — Claude-only on every row. His instructor asked him to read it back out loud. Step three: select the statistical method. Step five: set the classification threshold. He had written Claude-only for both. She asked: 'Do you know what method Claude will pick? Do you know if the threshold is right for this data?' He did not. This is Liam, in for Bear.

The Score table has five columns: step, phase, who does it, why, handoff condition. The who column has three values: Claude-only, human-only, and Claude-with-review. Claude-only means the step is purely mechanical and the outcome is verifiable. Human-only means the decision requires domain judgment that Claude cannot supply. Claude-with-review means Claude drafts, human checks before anything proceeds.
beats.new_visual_element: Score table — step / phase / who / why / handoff condition / three values for who

beats.narration_text: Brooks 1986 distinguished essential from accidental complexity. Accidental: the friction of building — syntax errors, boilerplate, scaffolding. Essential: deciding what to build, choosing the method, setting the threshold. Claude eats accidental complexity. Essential complexity belongs to the human. When you write Claude-only for a step that requires domain judgment, you have handed over the essential work and kept the accidental.
beats.new_visual_element: Essential complexity — Brooks 1986 / accidental: Claude / essential: human / method choice and threshold = essential
beats.spark_line: Claude eats accidental. You own essential.

beats.narration_text: A Score where every row says Claude-only is a signal: either the build is trivial, or you have quietly handed over the human work and cannot yet see it. The Score cannot lie — you can write a vague build description, but you cannot write a vague handoff condition without hearing the vagueness. 'Claude decides' is not a handoff condition. 'Classification threshold verified against baseline dataset' is.
beats.new_visual_element: All Claude-only = signal — trivial or dishonest / handoff condition cannot be vague / specific condition = test

beats.narration_text: The Score is not a judgment about capability — Claude is capable of many things. It is a prompt for your own judgment: which steps require a decision that only you can make? The Score forces that question before any code runs. A build with two human-only rows and three Claude-only rows is an honest build. A build with zero human-only rows is probably not.
beats.new_visual_element: Artifact view — completed Score table / human-only rows visible / honest build vs. all Claude-only
beats.spark_line: The Score is a prompt for your judgment.

beats.narration_text: Fill out the Score before your next build. Paste this.

props.command: Help me fill out a Boondoggle Score for this build: [describe it]. For each step, assign: (1) Who does it — Claude-only, human-only, or Claude-with-review, (2) Why — one sentence justification, (3) Handoff condition — the specific, checkable test that says this step is done. Flag any step I labeled Claude-only where the decision actually requires domain judgment I have not yet supplied.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [The Boondoggle Score: Label Who Does Each Step] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
